title: Soy botanical candle with dried flowers
subtitle: A 200 ml jar candle with pressed botanicals on the glass
excerpt: A soy wax jar candle with dried botanical flowers pressed against the inside of the glass before pouring. The flowers are visible through the glass wall and frame the burning candle. The technique requires a careful thin wax pour before positioning the botanicals.
sourceNotes: Bramble Berry botanical candle notes (brambleberry.com, May 2026), embedded-botanical technique and fire safety notes.

content.text: A soy wax jar candle with dried botanical flowers pressed against the inside of the glass. The flowers are embedded in a thin initial wax layer, which holds them in place; the main wax pour fills the rest of the jar. The candle reads as a glass-mounted botanical display when unlit and burns with the flowers framing the flame when lit.

A note on fire safety for botanical candles: keep the flowers pressed flat against the glass, not floating in the wax where they can contact the flame. Dry botanical material near a flame is a fire risk; the design works because the flowers are sealed in wax against the glass wall, not sitting loose near the wick. Never substitute fresh or semi-dried botanicals; only fully dried flowers work safely.

items.name: Soy wax (container blend)
items.prepNote: container-grade soy wax. 20-30 g for the first adhesion pour, remainder for the main fill pour.

items.name: Rose geranium essential oil
items.prepNote: rose geranium essential oil at 6% of wax weight. Or lavender, or any floral. Added at 55°C.

items.name: Candle wick, pre-tabbed (CD-12)
items.prepNote: pre-tabbed cotton wick sized for a 7 cm diameter jar.

content.text: Set the wick and prepare the botanicals

Adhere the wick tab to the centre base of the jar. Lay the centring bar across the top. Have the dried botanical flowers ready on a small plate; select flat flowers (pressed roses, lavender heads, dried cornflowers) that will sit flush against the glass.
First thin pour to adhere the botanicals

Melt a small amount of soy wax (20-30 g) and cool to 55-60°C. Pour a thin layer into the jar, just enough to cover the base and coat the glass sides to about 1 cm depth. Working quickly before the wax sets, press the dried botanicals against the inside glass wall using tweezers or a cocktail stick. The wax acts as the adhesive. When the botanicals are in position, allow this layer to set fully; approximately 30 minutes.

Melt the remaining wax (approximately 120 g) and cool to 55°C. Add the fragrance oil if using, stir for 2 minutes, then cool to 60-65°C. Pour the main fill into the jar, carefully and close to the surface to avoid disturbing the set botanicals. Fill to within 1 cm of the rim. Leave undisturbed 48 hours.
Trim the wick to 5 mm. Burn for 3-4 hours for the full

to the jar edge. Monitor the first burn: if any botanical material is not fully sealed in wax and comes loose, extinguish the candle and press the material back against the glass with a cocktail stick while the wax is soft.

Effectively indefinite unlit. Keep away from direct sunlight; UV fades the botanical colours and the essential oil fragrance. The botanicals retain their colour longest stored in a box away from light.
